string.text: Army Load-Bearing Vest
Load-Bearing Vest
A load-bearing vest designed for carrying essential ammunition and personal emergency medical supplies.
A belt pouch for carrying various small items.
Electronic Notebook
A reworked old smartphone turned into an electronic notebook by the technician Vergas.
Sleeping Bag
A lightweight sleeping bag. Waterproof. This model combines the comfort of a blanket-style bag with the high thermal insulation of a mummy cocoon.
Artistís Sketchbook
A sketchbook of a deceased artist, filled with drawings and travel notes.
Army Flask
A flask with a screw cap on a chain. Made of food-grade aluminum. Comes with a protective cover. Designed for convenient carrying of liquids.
Decoder
A homemade device for breaking into electronic door lock systems and extracting the keypad code matrix.
Belt Bag
A handy bag for storing a small number of minor items. Commonly used by stalkers as a reserve emergency stash.
Glass in a Holder
A glass found in the Sanatorium. Clearly old-fashioned.
Mug
A mug found in the Sanatorium.
Cup
A cup found in the Sanatorium.
Grail
A goblet found in the Sanatorium. Looks like tea was brewed in it more than once...
Weight with a Mug
A weight found in the Sanatorium. A full pood. How did a mug get embedded into it like that? Damn Zone!
Beer Mug
A beer mug found in the Sanatorium. Clearly been through an anomaly.
Skis
Old wooden skis with soft felt-boot bindings and bamboo poles. Donít glide at all.
Oh right, I found some kind of mug. Crawled all over the entire sanatorium...
Well Iíll be... Nice! Letís see it.
You take this mug. Iím sick of hauling it around.

And you werenít too lazy to lug all that weight, Marked One. The mug isnít mine, and I donít know the weight either... But you know, you moved me, Marked One. You didnít spare the time or effort, crawling through some ruins just to please an old man you barely know. Hand the thing over. Iíll keep it as a reminder of your good deed.

Thanks for the trouble, Marked One, but no hard feelings. Itís not mine... But you know, you moved me, Marked One. You didnít spare the time or effort, crawling through some ruins just to please an old man you barely know. Hand the thing over. Iíll keep it as a reminder of your good deed.

A fine little thing, but not mine. Use it yourself... But you know, you moved me, Marked One. You didnít spare the time or effort, crawling through some ruins just to please an old man you barely know. Hand the thing over. Iíll keep it as a reminder of your good deed.

Sorry, but thatís not what you were supposed to find. Maybe youíll get lucky next time... But you know, you moved me, Marked One. You didnít spare the time or effort, crawling through some ruins just to please an old man you barely know. Hand the thing over. Iíll keep it as a reminder of your good deed.

Donít recognize it. Someone elseís thing... But you know, you moved me, Marked One. You didnít spare the time or effort, crawling through some ruins just to please an old man you barely know. Hand the thing over. Iíll keep it as a reminder of your good deed.

Some kind of grail. You didnít find it in a church, did you? Head still aching? Clearly not mine... But you know, you moved me, Marked One. You didnít spare the time or effort, crawling through some ruins just to please an old man you barely know. Hand the thing over. Iíll keep it as a reminder of your good deed.
Here, take a look. Mustíve been one hell of a quake at the sanatorium if it got embedded into a weight like that...
Here. A beer mug. Clearly been through an anomaly. You can barely even see it...
Thatís not a cup, itís a glass. But itís got a holder.
Here, a mug like any other. Clearly someone drank tea from it.
Here it is, a little cup. Careful ó porcelain. Fine work.
Not quite a cup, but they clearly had tea with it.
Oh! Thatís mine! Hand it over, weíll carve it out of the weight.\nAnd special thanks for not selling it to anyone.
Mine, my favorite! Holds as much as a samovar.\nAnd special thanks for not selling it to anyone.
My little glass! I remember nicking it on a train once... Accidentally.\nAnd special thanks for not selling it to anyone.
Thatís the one, dear to my heart. You did me a real favor, Marked One.\nAnd special thanks for not selling it to anyone.
A collectorís item. Thatís what I used to drink tea from at the sanatorium.\nAnd special thanks for not selling it to anyone.
There it is, my imperial goblet!\nAnd special thanks for not selling it to anyone.
Well then, take it and enjoy!
Iíll give this to you, VergasÖ a bit later, I willÖ

Thank you, Marked One. At least Iíll have some kind of keepsake. Of my past life, or of your good deed ó all that matters is that it warms the soul.\nAnd this is for you. Been saving it, so to speak, for old age, but for you ó no regrets.
Well damnÖ And what do I need skis for? Itís May.
Nope, I donít need a gun like that. Keep it.
Donít need a sleeping bag ó extra weight.
Thanks, I already have one. Donít need a second pair of binoculars.
A batteryís a good thing, but Iíve got nowhere to put it right now. Sorry.
Iíll manage without that belt.
Iíd take the container, but itís too heavy. Keep it.
I donít use that kind of ammo. No thanks.
Keep the repair kits. You need them yourself.
These medkits are too weak. Got any army or scientific ones?
Donít need matches right now. A lighter would be better.
Nice armor, but mineís better. Iíll pass.
Donít need this. Field rations give me heartburn.
Vodkaís a good thing, but not right now.
Grenades are kinda weak. Got any anti-tank ones?
Donít need antirads. Iíll treat myself with vodka instead.
Donít need those ìcatís eyesî. Heard they make your kidneys fail.
How do people even drink this? Wonít even go down. Vodkaís better.
Canned food again... No, thanks.
Hunting? No need.
The best sausage is a sock full of money. Wanna trade for that?
You shouldnít have passed on that. Itís a good piece.
A sleeping bagís a solid thing. Youíll regret it later, when itís too late.
EhÖ that optics is German, you know.
A batteryÖ That batteryís worth its weight in gold.
Just so you know, belts like that donít lie around on the roadside.
Couldíve dropped it off in Stasís stash, saved it for better times.
Youíre a strange one, Marked One. You could trade that for anything you want at the Bar.
True enough. Youíll come crawling back for repairs anyway.
Medkits like these are a real rarity around here.
Look at what youíre asking for. Gasolineís in short supply these days.
Wouldnít say so myself, but suit yourself.
Agreed. This is nowhere near a NATO field rationÖ
Up to you. Drink vodka then ó surprise your liver.
Maybe youíd rather I hook you up with a gun?
A decent option, but antiradís more reliable. In my opinion.
Your loss. Thatís good chemistry. And everyoneís liverís still intact so far.
Right on the mark. But sometimes a can like that saves your life.
As you say. Canned food tastes like crap, but at least it doesnít spoil.
Thatís where youíre wrong. Tasty, and it wonít go bad either.
Youíll earn it yourself. All I can offer is a sock.
Thanks. Hope this comes in handy.
Youíre welcome.
Alright, Iím off.
Hey now, my friend, only the Zone knows how long youíll be walking her paths. Youíll probably ring in the New Year somewhere around here.
Donít jinx it, Vergas. But fine ó Iíll take it.
There we go.
Got it. Iím not an idiot.

Vergas, I canít get your story out of my headÖ the one about you lying paralyzed in that local Sanatorium, then the emission putting you back on your feet. You also asked me to look for your cup, remember? Itís all strange. What if there are sometimes special, ìhealingî emissions?

ìHealing,î yeah right. It only worked like that on me ó for the others lying there, it was hell. And why it happened to me that way, who knows. And the cupÖ you know, Iím starting to think maybe there was no ìspecialî cup at all. Just an ordinary hospital one. Or maybe I made it up entirely, just so life wouldnít feel so scary. Just to have at least some proof of my past life.

Yeah, I get it. Iíd dig with my bare hands just to learn anything at all about my own past. By the way, about your cup ó Iíve been to that Sanatorium more than once, but I never came across any dishes. Except maybe three-liter jars.

Really? Thatís a shame. Youíre the one who took care of the burer that settled there, right? The youngsters have been talkingÖ Youíre a good man, Marked One. You didnít forget my request, and you even did a good deed that helped everyone. Ah, to hell with it! Iíd been saving something for a rainy day, but you need it more. Here, take this ó a gift from me.
Well, old manÖ I donít even know what to say. I didnít expect this. Thank you.
